response: TEMPERATURE IS A MEASUREMENT OF THE HEAT ENERGY IN A SUBSTANCE. IT IS A KEY FACTOR IN MANY PHYSICAL AND CHEMICAL PROCESSES. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SOLUTION CAN AFFECT THE RATE OF REACTIONS. WHEN A SOLUTION IS COOLED, THE TEMPERATURE DECREASES. THE TEMPERATURE OF A BOWL OF WATER CAN BE MEASURED WITH A TEMPERATURE SENSOR. THE TEMPERATURE OF A ROOM IS A COMMON REFERENCE POINT FOR TEMPERATURE MEASUREMENT. THE TEMPERATURE OF A BODY IS RELATIVE TO ITS SURROUNDINGS. THE TEMPERATURE OF A MATERIAL CAN CHANGE WITH TIME. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SYSTEM CAN BE CHANGED BY ADDING OR REMOVING HEAT. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SOLUTION CAN AFFECT THE RATE OF REACTIONS. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SOLUTION CAN AFFECT THE RATE OF REACTIONS.ROLL IS A KEY FACTOR IN MANY CHEMICAL REACTIONS.
